prompt: A premium artisan food photography image of a clear acrylic collector box displayed on a rustic wooden table, shot in a clean 4:5 product style with soft natural light and shallow depth of field. The box contains a miniature kitchen diorama dedicated to Eggs Benedict, with a tiny chef figurine in a white toque and white uniform with a red neckerchief standing at center, mid-preparation, pouring glossy hollandaise sauce over a stacked eggs benedict on an English muffin half. Inside the center kitchen area, include 1 miniature stove, 1 frying pan, 1 small saucepan in the chef’s hand, 1 wooden prep table, 2 knives on the table, 3 hanging metal utensils, 2 stacked plates on the lower shelf, 1 small white bowl, and 2 seasoning mills beneath the table. Surround the center scene with exactly 12 clear ingredient compartments built into the acrylic box walls: top row has 4 compartments containing 2 English muffins, 3 butter cubes, 3 lemon slices, and 3 whole eggs; left side has 4 compartments containing 3 round slices of Canadian bacon, 2 sprigs of parsley, 1 whole egg, and 2 condiment bottles; right side has 4 compartments containing 1 origin map card labeled USA with a red map of the United States and a tiny NYC mark, 1 compartment of chopped chives, 2 spice jars, and 3 cracked egg yolks. The acrylic packaging has bold red, white, and blue trim bands around the top and bottom edges, evoking American flag colors. Add a front label plaque reading EGGS BENEDICT with smaller text USA - 1894. Keep the miniature highly detailed, realistic, polished, and luxurious, like a museum-grade culinary collectible or upscale toy packaging, with crisp transparent reflections and a warm editorial tabletop background.
